title: The entailment symbol ⊢ rendered as heavy geometric slabs rather than text. ⊢ means 'proves' or 'derives' — the definition on the left proves all valid configurations on the right. Precept's .precept file is the premise; every valid state is a structurally necessary consequence. The heavy slab construction gives the symbol maximum architectural weight — this is not a notation mark but a structural claim.
conceptualBridge: The slab entailment mark means Precept's definition derives all valid states with mathematical necessity because ⊢ is the formal symbol for 'proves from' — the contract is the left side and every reachable configuration follows from it.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.44 avg — the heavy slab construction gives the ⊢ symbol maximum structural authority; perfect legibility and monochrome scores; warm saffron palette is new to the active set; different enough from 29-entailment (text) to earn its own slot.
